items.question: We ___ a great film last night.
explanations: 'Watch' is the base form. 'Last night' is a finished time, so we need the past simple: 'watched'.
Correct! Regular verbs add -ed in the past simple: 'watched'. 'Last night' signals a finished time.
'Watches' is present simple (he/she/it). A finished past action takes 'watched'.
'Were watching' (past continuous) describes an action in progress. For a completed action, use 'watched'.

items.question: She ___ to Paris in 2019.
explanations: 'Go' is irregular, so it doesn't add -ed. The past form is 'went', not 'goed'.
Correct! 'Go' is irregular: its past simple form is 'went'.
'Gone' is the past participle (used with have/has). The past simple is 'went'.
'Going' needs an auxiliary. A finished action in 2019 takes 'went'.

items.question: They ___ the message.
explanations: After 'didn't' the verb goes back to its base form: 'see', not 'saw'.
Correct! Negatives use didn't + base verb: 'didn't see'. 'Did' already carries the past.
'Don't' is present simple, and 'saw' can't follow it. The past negative is 'didn't see'.
English needs the auxiliary 'did' in the negative: 'didn't see', not just 'not saw'.

items.question: ___ you ___ the news this morning?
explanations: Correct! Past questions use Did + subject + base verb: 'Did you hear…?'.
After 'did' the verb is the base form: 'hear', not 'heard'.
'Do' is present simple, and it can't combine with 'heard'. Use 'Did you hear?'.
'Have you heard' is present perfect. With 'this morning' as finished time, the past simple fits: 'Did you hear?'.

items.question: I ___ him at the station yesterday.
explanations: 'Yesterday' is a finished time, so the present perfect is impossible here. Use 'saw'.
Correct! A finished-time expression like 'yesterday' always takes the past simple: 'saw'.
'Has seen' doesn't agree with 'I', and 'yesterday' needs the past simple anyway.
'See' is the base present form. A completed action yesterday takes 'saw'.

items.question: He ___ a new car last month.
explanations: 'Buy' is irregular, so it doesn't add -ed. The past is 'bought', not 'buyed'.
Correct! 'Buy' is irregular: the past simple is 'bought'.
'Buy' is the base form. 'Last month' is finished time, so use 'bought'.
'Buys' is present simple. A finished action last month takes 'bought'.

items.question: The past simple of 'study' is ___.
explanations: When a verb ends in consonant + -y, the -y changes to -ied: not 'studyed'.
Correct! Consonant + -y becomes -ied: study → studied.
'Studed' drops too much. The rule is -y → -ied: studied.
'Studies' is present simple (he/she/it). The past simple is 'studied'.

items.question: We ___ in Girona when we were children.
explanations: 'Live' is the base form. A finished past state takes the past simple: 'lived'.
Correct! A past state that is now finished uses the past simple: 'lived'.
'Have lived' (present perfect) suggests it continues now, but 'when we were children' is finished.
'Are living' is present continuous, about now. The finished past takes 'lived'.

items.question: Rewrite with the same meaning: 'It's ten years since I last saw her.'
options: I last saw her ten years ago.
I haven't seen her for ten years ago.
I have seen her ten years ago.
I didn't see her ten years ago.
explanations: Correct! 'It's ten years since I last saw her' = 'I last saw her ten years ago' — a finished action with 'ago' takes the past simple.
'For … ago' mixes two structures and isn't correct English.
'Ago' marks finished time, so it can't go with the present perfect 'have seen'.
'Didn't see' means the meeting never happened, but the original says it did — ten years ago.

items.question: When the phone rang, I ___ it immediately.
explanations: 'Was answering' suggests an action already in progress, but answering was a quick reaction to the ring.
Correct! Two completed past actions in sequence (rang, then answered) both take the past simple.
'Answer' is the base present form. The action happened in the past: 'answered'.
'Have answered' is present perfect. With a finished past sequence, use the past simple: 'answered'.
